title: Brief
description: The dispatchable unit of work. The brief is the compression boundary: frontier judgment goes in, so cheaper tiers can execute what comes out. A brief with outcome-free acceptance gates is invalid by doctrine, not just by taste.

statement.description: What is wrong or missing, as observed — not as remembered.

causal_chain.description: Step-by-step: from trigger to symptom. Each step falsifiable.

evidence.description: The demonstration that pinned it: repro, bisect, byte-level diff...

root_cause.description: MANDATORY when kind=bug (rigid step 2), enforced by the if/then clause on `problem` — not just this description. A fix without this does not enter implement-and-gate.

approach.description: The design, in enough detail that a mid-tier implementer needs no further judgment calls.

touch_points.description: Files/modules to change, path per entry, with a phrase on what changes there.

rejected_alternatives.description: A decision names the rejected alternative — or it was not a decision (prospekt invariant).

id.description: Stable within the brief (G1, G2...). Never renumbered.

statement.description: What must be TRUE, phrased as an outcome: 'the timer FIRES and the run completes with slept' — never 'a TimerStarted event exists'.

kind.description: command = exit-status activity; outcome_test = test asserting forward progress; live_operator = truth-pass row.

asserts.description: Shape-assertions are not a legal value on purpose.

command.description: For kind=command: the exact command whose OWN exit status judges the gate.

out_of_scope.description: Explicit non-goals. The implementer treats crossing these as a stop-and-surface event.

refutation_survived.description: Reference to the refutation report this design survived (set by the brief-forge workflow, not the designer).
